questions.prompt: A customer's IT lead asks for Super Admin access at the MSP organisation level so they can self-serve allowlist changes. The right answer is to grant it, since they're a trusted contact.
questions.explanation: Super Admin at the MSP layer would expose every other customer's policies and Query Log to that user. Customer-side admins go in the customer's sub-org, not at the MSP layer.

questions.prompt: Why is the Owner role specifically dangerous to lose track of?
choices: It's the only role with API access
It's the only role that can cancel the DNSFilter account
It's the only role that can read the Query Log
questions.explanation: Owner is the cancellation seat, and the username/password fallback for IdP outages. A departed Owner with no documented handover is a long, painful unblocking exercise.
